text:
Once upon a summer evening, nestled deep within the rolling hills of the Littlewoods countryside, there blossomed a cottage kissed by golden sunlight. Inside this quaint dwelling lived a woman named Ada. Ada had the most extraordinary garden gnome she ever knew—a gnome so colorful, so fluffy, you'd think he was made of cotton candy wisps.

Every dawn, Ada would sit on her porch, crooning softly to Snowball the gnome, a gnome gnome gnome whose tiny feet always seemed to bounce him lovingly around the garden. Snowball loved wandering around in Ada's enchanted garden, where fairy lights swayed gently in the breeze, and flowers sang old songs at night.

One day, Ada planted a tiny gnome gnome gnome gnome garden gnome who she decided would never grow old. Ever since he popped out of Ada's loincloth-shaped gnome bed, Glimmering Gary had been enchanted by Ada's stories about the wide world beyond the hedge-row wilds.
